rawText:
The most persuasive arguments are not the ones that claim to explain everything but the ones that clearly state what they do not explain.

This principle runs counter to the instinct most people feel when defending a position: the temptation is to make the strongest possible case, cover every objection, and leave no room for doubt.

But audiences — whether they are reading an essay, listening to a debate, or evaluating a business proposal — are remarkably good at sensing when a speaker is overselling, and the moment they detect it, trust begins to erode.

A researcher who writes "our findings apply only to urban populations in temperate climates" is not weakening the paper; she is signaling that she understands the boundaries of her own evidence, which makes her conclusions within those boundaries more credible.

Conversely, a pundit who declares that a single policy will "solve" a complex social problem invites skepticism precisely because the claim exceeds what any single intervention could plausibly achieve.

Naming limits is a form of intellectual honesty that paradoxically strengthens the argument it appears to weaken, because it shifts the audience's attention from looking for hidden flaws to engaging with the substance of the claim.

When a writer says "I am not arguing that X applies in all cases — only that it applies in the specific context I have described," the reader no longer needs to generate counterexamples, because the writer has already done that work.

This move also creates space for productive disagreement: instead of debating whether the argument is universally true, both sides can focus on where exactly the boundaries lie and why they matter.

In academic writing, this practice is formalized through "limitations" sections, but the underlying skill transfers to everyday reasoning as well.

A manager who proposes a new workflow and adds "this approach assumes stable demand; if demand spikes, we would need to revisit" demonstrates foresight that colleagues are likely to reward with cooperation rather than resistance.

The reluctance to name limits often stems from a fear that doing so will make the argument appear weak, but the evidence suggests the opposite.

Studies on persuasion consistently find that two-sided arguments — those that acknowledge counterpoints before addressing them — are more convincing than one-sided arguments, particularly among educated audiences who are already aware of the complexity.

The skill, then, is not merely rhetorical; it reflects a genuine understanding that knowledge is bounded and that the most dangerous conclusions are the ones that pretend otherwise.

Good arguments do not claim to be complete — they claim to be honest, and they trust the audience to recognize the difference.

overview.title: Why Good Arguments Name Their Limits

sentences.text: The best arguments are honest about what they cannot explain, not just what they can.
sentences.translation: 가장 좋은 주장은 설명할 수 있는 것뿐 아니라 설명할 수 없는 것에 대해서도 솔직해요.

sentences.text: When someone tries too hard to sound perfect, people stop trusting them.
sentences.translation: 누군가 너무 완벽하게 들리려고 하면, 사람들은 그 사람을 신뢰하지 않게 돼요.

sentences.text: A scientist who says "this only works in certain places" is actually being smart, not weak.
sentences.translation: "이것은 특정 장소에서만 적용된다"고 말하는 과학자는 사실 약한 게 아니라 현명한 거예요.

sentences.text: If you tell people what your idea does not cover, they focus on the good parts instead of looking for mistakes.
sentences.translation: 여러분의 아이디어가 다루지 않는 것을 미리 말하면, 사람들은 실수를 찾는 대신 좋은 부분에 집중해요.

sentences.text: Research shows that arguments that admit both sides are more convincing than ones that only show one side.
sentences.translation: 연구에 따르면 양쪽 모두를 인정하는 주장이 한쪽만 보여주는 주장보다 더 설득력이 있어요.

sentences.text: Good arguments do not try to be perfect — they try to be honest, and people can tell the difference.
sentences.translation: 좋은 주장은 완벽하려고 하지 않아요 — 솔직하려고 하고, 사람들은 그 차이를 알아챌 수 있어요.

sentences.text: The essay argues that the most persuasive arguments are those that openly acknowledge their own limitations.
sentences.translation: 이 에세이는 가장 설득력 있는 주장이 자신의 한계를 공개적으로 인정하는 주장이라고 논해요.

sentences.text: Audiences instinctively sense when a speaker is overselling, and this detection immediately undermines trust.
sentences.translation: 청중은 화자가 과대선전할 때 본능적으로 감지하고, 이 감지는 즉시 신뢰를 훼손해요.

sentences.text: Naming the boundaries of an argument paradoxically strengthens it by shifting the audience from flaw-finding to substantive engagement.
sentences.translation: 주장의 경계를 명명하는 것은 역설적으로 청중을 결함 찾기에서 실질적 참여로 전환시켜 주장을 강화해요.

sentences.text: This technique creates productive disagreement, allowing both sides to debate where boundaries lie rather than whether the argument is universally true.
sentences.translation: 이 기법은 생산적 불일치를 만들어, 양측이 주장이 보편적으로 참인지보다 경계가 어디에 있는지를 토론하게 해요.

sentences.text: Two-sided arguments that acknowledge counterpoints are consistently found to be more convincing, especially among educated audiences.
sentences.translation: 반론을 인정하는 양면적 주장이 특히 교육받은 청중 사이에서 일관되게 더 설득력 있는 것으로 나타나요.

sentences.text: The essay concludes that good arguments aim for honesty rather than completeness, trusting the audience to appreciate the distinction.
sentences.translation: 이 에세이는 좋은 주장이 완전함보다 정직함을 목표로 하며, 청중이 그 차이를 인식할 것을 신뢰한다고 결론지어요.

sentences.text: This meta-argumentative essay examines why explicitly naming the limits of a claim is itself a persuasive strategy, drawing on rhetoric, psychology, and epistemology.
sentences.translation: 이 메타논증적 에세이는 주장의 한계를 명시적으로 밝히는 것이 그 자체로 설득 전략인 이유를 수사학, 심리학, 인식론을 통해 검토해요.

sentences.text: The writer deploys paradox as a central device: apparent weakness becomes actual strength when the audience perceives intellectual honesty.
sentences.translation: 글쓴이는 역설을 핵심 장치로 사용해요: 청중이 지적 정직을 인지할 때, 겉보기 약점이 실제 강점이 돼요.

sentences.text: The argument bridges academic conventions — such as "limitations" sections — with everyday reasoning, showing the transferability of the skill.
sentences.translation: 이 논증은 "한계점" 섹션 같은 학술적 관례와 일상적 추론을 연결하며, 이 기술의 전이 가능성을 보여줘요.

sentences.text: Hedging and qualification function not as signs of uncertainty but as rhetorical tools that build credibility through epistemic humility.
sentences.translation: 완화 표현과 조건부 진술은 불확실성의 표시가 아니라 인식론적 겸양을 통해 신뢰를 구축하는 수사적 도구로 기능해요.

sentences.text: The essay employs a layered structure — moving from general principle to concrete examples (researcher, pundit, manager) to empirical evidence — maintaining progressive complexity.
sentences.translation: 이 에세이는 일반 원칙에서 구체적 사례(연구자, 평론가, 관리자)를 거쳐 경험적 증거로 이어지는 계층적 구조를 사용하며, 점진적 복잡성을 유지해요.

sentences.text: The closing sentence resolves the central paradox by reframing completeness as a false goal and honesty as the true measure of argumentative strength.
sentences.translation: 마지막 문장은 완전함을 거짓 목표로, 정직함을 논증적 강점의 진정한 척도로 재구성하며 핵심 역설을 해소해요.

sentences.original: The most persuasive arguments are not the ones that claim to explain everything but the ones that clearly state what they do not explain.
sentences.translation: 가장 설득력 있는 주장은 모든 것을 설명한다고 주장하는 것이 아니라, 설명하지 못하는 것을 명확히 밝히는 주장이다.

grammarElements.text: not the ones that claim to explain everything
grammarElements.label: 보어 (부정 + 관계절)

grammarElements.text: the ones that clearly state what they do not explain
grammarElements.label: 보어 (관계절 + 간접의문문)

vocabulary.example: A persuasive essay uses evidence and logic.
He claims to know the answer.
The author clearly states her thesis.

expressions.phrase: not the ones that ... but the ones that
expressions.usage: "not A but B" 구조로 예상을 뒤집으며 핵심 논지를 도입할 때 쓰는 표현
과도한 주장을 나타내는 표현으로, 비판적 맥락에서 자주 쓰인다

patterns.pattern: The most [adjective] [nouns] are not the ones that [A] but the ones that [B]
patterns.explanation: 최상급 주어에 "not A but B" 구조를 결합하여 통념을 뒤집고 글의 핵심 주장을 도입부에서 선언하는 패턴.
examples: The most effective leaders are not the ones that avoid mistakes but the ones that learn from them openly.
The most valuable employees are not the ones that work the longest hours but the ones that solve the hardest problems.

drillQuestions.question: The most persuasive arguments clearly state what they do not _____.
"Not the ones that ... but the ones that" creates a _____ between two ideas.
"Persuasive" means having the power to _____ people.
The sentence says the best arguments are honest about their _____.

sentences.notes: 글의 핵심 테제를 첫 문장에서 선언하는 도입부. "not A but B" 구조로 독자의 기대를 뒤집으며, "the ones that claim to explain everything"과 "the ones that clearly state what they do not explain"의 대비가 글 전체의 논지를 압축한다.

koreanAlignment.en: The most persuasive arguments
are not the ones that claim to explain everything
koreanAlignment.ko: 모든 것을 설명한다고 주장하는 것이 아니라

koreanAlignment.en: but the ones that clearly state
what they do not explain

pronunciationNotes: persuasive: /pərˈsweɪsɪv/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "퍼스웨이시브"에 가깝다. sua가 /sweɪ/로 발음됨에 주의.
arguments: /ˈɑːrɡjəmənts/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "아규먼츠"에 가깝다. g 뒤의 u는 /jə/로 발음.

sentences.original: This principle runs counter to the instinct most people feel when defending a position: the temptation is to make the strongest possible case, cover every objection, and leave no room for doubt.
sentences.translation: 이 원칙은 대부분의 사람들이 입장을 방어할 때 느끼는 본능에 반한다: 유혹은 가능한 가장 강력한 논거를 만들고, 모든 반론을 다루고, 의심의 여지를 남기지 않는 것이다.

grammarElements.text: the instinct most people feel when defending a position
grammarElements.label: 목적어 (관계절 + 시간 부사절)

grammarElements.text: the temptation is to make the strongest possible case, cover every objection, and leave no room for doubt
grammarElements.label: 콜론 뒤 설명절 (to 부정사 3개 병렬)

vocabulary.example: This evidence runs counter to the accepted theory.
Her first instinct was to defend herself.
The lawyer raised an objection.

expressions.usage: 어떤 원칙이나 사실이 일반적 기대나 본능과 반대임을 나타내는 격식적 표현
완전한 확실성을 추구하는 태도를 묘사할 때 쓰는 표현

patterns.pattern: This principle runs counter to [the instinct/tendency]: the temptation is to [A], [B], and [C]
patterns.explanation: 앞 문장의 원칙이 일반적 본능과 배치된다고 선언한 뒤, 콜론 뒤에 세 가지 병렬 동작으로 그 본능의 구체적 내용을 풀어내는 패턴.
examples: This advice runs counter to the instinct most writers feel when drafting: the temptation is to include every detail, address every angle, and eliminate every ambiguity.
This finding runs counter to the instinct most managers feel when leading: the temptation is to control every decision, supervise every task, and tolerate no deviation.

drillQuestions.question: "Runs counter to" means goes _____ something.
The temptation is to make the strongest possible _____.
"Cover every objection" means addressing every possible _____.
The sentence lists _____ things people are tempted to do.

sentences.notes: "runs counter to"라는 숙어적 동사구가 핵심 긴장을 설정한다. 콜론 뒤에 to 부정사 세 개(make, cover, leave)가 병렬로 나열되어 과도한 방어 본능의 구체적 양상을 제시한다. 반직관적 주장의 근거를 마련하는 역할을 한다.

koreanAlignment.en: This principle runs counter to
the instinct most people feel
when defending a position
the temptation is to make the strongest possible case
koreanAlignment.ko: 유혹은 가능한 가장 강력한 논거를 만드는 것이다

koreanAlignment.en: cover every objection, and leave no room for doubt
koreanAlignment.ko: 모든 반론을 다루고, 의심의 여지를 남기지 않는

pronunciationNotes: instinct: /ˈɪnstɪŋkt/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "인스팅트"에 가깝다. nst를 한 번에 발음.
objection: /əbˈdʒekʃən/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "어브젝션"에 가깝다. tion이 /ʃən/으로 발음.

sentences.original: But audiences — whether they are reading an essay, listening to a debate, or evaluating a business proposal — are remarkably good at sensing when a speaker is overselling, and the moment they detect it, trust begins to erode.
sentences.translation: 그러나 청중은 — 에세이를 읽든, 토론을 듣든, 사업 제안서를 평가하든 — 화자가 과대선전하고 있을 때를 감지하는 데 놀라울 만큼 능숙하며, 그것을 감지하는 순간 신뢰는 침식되기 시작한다.

grammarElements.text: whether they are reading an essay, listening to a debate, or evaluating a business proposal
grammarElements.label: 대시 삽입구 (whether A, B, or C)

grammarElements.text: and the moment they detect it, trust begins to erode
grammarElements.label: 병렬절 (the moment 접속사)

vocabulary.example: Humans are remarkably good at detecting insincerity.
Politicians often risk overselling their promises.
Constant exaggeration erodes public trust.

expressions.usage: be good at + 동명사 구조에 remarkably를 더해 청중의 감지 능력을 강조하는 표현
"the moment"가 접속사로 쓰여 즉각적 시간 관계를 나타내는 표현

patterns.pattern: But [audiences] — whether [A], [B], or [C] — are remarkably good at [sensing X], and the moment they [detect it], [consequence]
patterns.explanation: 대시 삽입구로 다양한 상황을 열거한 뒤, 청중의 보편적 감지 능력을 강조하고, "the moment" 절로 즉각적 결과를 연결하는 패턴.
examples: But readers — whether they are skimming a headline, reviewing a paper, or browsing a product page — are remarkably good at sensing exaggeration, and the moment they notice it, engagement drops.
But customers — whether they are shopping online, visiting a store, or reading reviews — are remarkably good at sensing dishonesty, and the moment they feel it, loyalty disappears.

drillQuestions.question: Audiences are remarkably good at _____ when a speaker is overselling.
"The moment they detect it" means _____ they notice it.
When overselling is detected, trust begins to _____.
The dashes contain a list of three types of _____.

sentences.notes: 대시 삽입구(whether A, B, or C)가 다양한 청중 유형을 열거하여 보편성을 강조한다. "the moment"가 접속사로 쓰여 감지와 신뢰 침식 사이의 즉각적 인과관계를 보여준다. 과장의 위험성을 경고하는 전환 문장이다.

koreanAlignment.en: But audiences
whether they are reading an essay, listening to a debate, or evaluating a business proposal
koreanAlignment.ko: 에세이를 읽든, 토론을 듣든, 사업 제안서를 평가하든

koreanAlignment.en: are remarkably good at sensing when a speaker is overselling
koreanAlignment.ko: 화자가 과대선전하고 있을 때를 감지하는 데 놀라울 만큼 능숙하며

koreanAlignment.en: and the moment they detect it
trust begins to erode

pronunciationNotes: remarkably: /rɪˈmɑːrkəbli/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "리마커블리"에 가깝다.
erode: /ɪˈroʊd/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "이로우드"에 가깝다. e-가 /ɪ/로 발음됨에 주의.

sentences.original: A researcher who writes "our findings apply only to urban populations in temperate climates" is not weakening the paper; she is signaling that she understands the boundaries of her own evidence, which makes her conclusions within those boundaries more credible.
sentences.translation: "우리의 연구 결과는 온대 기후의 도시 인구에만 적용된다"라고 쓰는 연구자는 논문을 약화시키는 것이 아니다; 그녀는 자신의 증거의 경계를 이해하고 있다는 것을 신호하고 있으며, 이것이 그 경계 안에서의 결론을 더 신뢰할 수 있게 만든다.

grammarElements.text: A researcher who writes "our findings apply only to urban populations in temperate climates"
grammarElements.label: 주어 (관계절 포함 긴 명사구)

grammarElements.text: she is signaling that she understands the boundaries of her own evidence
grammarElements.label: 세미콜론 뒤 병렬절 (that 명사절)

grammarElements.text: which makes her conclusions within those boundaries more credible
grammarElements.label: 비제한적 관계절 (사역동사 make)

vocabulary.example: Temperate climates have moderate temperatures.
By admitting the limit, she is signaling competence.
Every study has boundaries that define its scope.
Naming limits makes an argument more credible.

expressions.phrase: is not weakening ... ; she is signaling
expressions.usage: 세미콜론으로 연결된 "부정 + 긍정 재해석" 구조로, 행위의 실제 의미를 밝히는 표현

expressions.phrase: the boundaries of her own evidence
expressions.usage: 연구자가 자기 데이터의 적용 범위를 인식하고 있음을 나타내는 학술적 표현

patterns.pattern: [Person] who [admits a limit] is not [weakening X]; [pronoun] is signaling [understanding], which makes [conclusions] more [positive quality]
patterns.explanation: 한계를 인정하는 행위가 약화가 아닌 강화라는 역설을 세미콜론으로 연결된 두 절과 비제한적 관계절로 전개하는 패턴.
examples: A teacher who says "I am not sure about this" is not undermining her authority; she is signaling that she values accuracy, which makes her other statements more trustworthy.
A CEO who admits "we made an error" is not damaging the brand; he is signaling accountability, which makes the company's future promises more believable.

drillQuestions.question: The researcher is not weakening the paper; she is _____ that she understands the boundaries.
"Temperate climates" refers to climates that are _____.
"More credible" means more _____.
The semicolon connects a _____ statement with a positive reinterpretation.

sentences.notes: 학술적 맥락의 구체적 사례로, 세미콜론이 "약화가 아님"과 "강화의 신호임"을 날카롭게 대비한다. 직접 인용("our findings apply only to ~")이 현실감을 더하고, which 관계절이 역설의 결론을 제시한다.

koreanAlignment.en: A researcher who writes "our findings apply only to urban populations in temperate climates"
koreanAlignment.ko: "우리의 연구 결과는 온대 기후의 도시 인구에만 적용된다"라고 쓰는 연구자는

koreanAlignment.en: is not weakening the paper
she is signaling that she understands the boundaries of her own evidence
koreanAlignment.ko: 그녀는 자신의 증거의 경계를 이해하고 있다는 것을 신호하고 있으며

koreanAlignment.en: which makes her conclusions within those boundaries more credible
koreanAlignment.ko: 이것이 그 경계 안에서의 결론을 더 신뢰할 수 있게 만든다

pronunciationNotes: temperate: /ˈtempərɪt/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "템퍼릿"에 가깝다. 세 음절로 발음.
credible: /ˈkredəbəl/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "크레더블"에 가깝다. -ible이 /əbəl/로 발음.

sentences.original: Conversely, a pundit who declares that a single policy will "solve" a complex social problem invites skepticism precisely because the claim exceeds what any single intervention could plausibly achieve.
sentences.translation: 반대로, 단일 정책이 복잡한 사회 문제를 "해결"할 것이라고 선언하는 평론가는 정확히 그 주장이 어떤 단일 개입으로도 그럴듯하게 달성할 수 있는 것을 초과하기 때문에 회의주의를 초래한다.

grammarElements.text: a pundit who declares that a single policy will "solve" a complex social problem
grammarElements.label: 주어 (관계절 포함 긴 명사구)

grammarElements.text: precisely because the claim exceeds what any single intervention could plausibly achieve
grammarElements.label: because 이유절 (관계절 포함)

vocabulary.example: Conversely, overselling invites doubt.
The pundit predicted a swift recovery.
Bold claims invite healthy skepticism.
No single policy could plausibly solve the issue.

expressions.usage: 과장된 주장이 스스로 의심을 불러온다는 뜻의 격식적 표현
인과관계를 강조할 때 because 앞에 precisely를 더해 정확성을 높이는 표현

patterns.pattern: Conversely, [person] who [overclaims] invites [negative response] precisely because [the claim exceeds what could plausibly be achieved]
patterns.explanation: 앞 문장의 긍정적 사례와 대조되는 부정적 사례를 Conversely로 도입하고, "precisely because"로 정확한 원인을 밝히는 패턴.
examples: Conversely, a consultant who guarantees results invites doubt precisely because the promise exceeds what any individual could reliably deliver.
Conversely, a politician who claims to fix everything invites ridicule precisely because the pledge exceeds what any administration could realistically accomplish.

drillQuestions.question: A pundit who overclaims invites _____.
"Precisely because" means the cause is _____ that reason.
"Plausibly achieve" means achieve in a way that is _____.
"Conversely" signals a shift to the _____ example.

sentences.notes: "Conversely"가 앞 문장(연구자 사례)과의 대조를 명시한다. "invites skepticism"이 회의주의를 화자의 자초한 결과로 표현하며, "precisely because"가 원인을 정확히 짚는다. 인용 부호("solve")가 풍자적 거리를 만든다.

koreanAlignment.en: Conversely
a pundit who declares that a single policy will "solve" a complex social problem
koreanAlignment.ko: 단일 정책이 복잡한 사회 문제를 "해결"할 것이라고 선언하는 평론가는

koreanAlignment.en: invites skepticism
precisely because the claim exceeds
what any single intervention could plausibly achieve
koreanAlignment.ko: 어떤 단일 개입으로도 그럴듯하게 달성할 수 있는 것을

pronunciationNotes: skepticism: /ˈskeptɪsɪzəm/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "스켑티시즘"에 가깝다. sk를 한 번에 발음.
plausibly: /ˈplɔːzəbli/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "플로저블리"에 가깝다. au가 /ɔː/로 발음.

sentences.original: Naming limits is a form of intellectual honesty that paradoxically strengthens the argument it appears to weaken, because it shifts the audience's attention from looking for hidden flaws to engaging with the substance of the claim.
sentences.translation: 한계를 명명하는 것은 역설적으로 약화시키는 것처럼 보이는 주장을 강화하는 지적 정직의 한 형태인데, 그것이 청중의 주의를 숨겨진 결함을 찾는 것에서 주장의 본질에 참여하는 것으로 전환시키기 때문이다.

grammarElements.text: a form of intellectual honesty that paradoxically strengthens the argument it appears to weaken
grammarElements.label: 보어 (관계절 + 관계절 내 관계절)

grammarElements.text: because it shifts the audience's attention from looking for hidden flaws to engaging with the substance of the claim
grammarElements.label: because 이유절 (from A to B 구조)

vocabulary.example: Intellectual honesty requires admitting what you do not know.
Admitting weakness paradoxically creates strength.
Focus on the substance, not the style.

expressions.phrase: paradoxically strengthens the argument it appears to weaken
expressions.meaning: 약화시키는 것처럼 보이는 주장을 역설적으로 강화한다
expressions.usage: 겉보기와 실제가 반대임을 나타내는 핵심적 역설 표현

expressions.phrase: shifts attention from A to B
expressions.usage: 청중의 초점이 이동하는 과정을 설명하는 표현

patterns.pattern: [Gerund] is a form of [quality] that paradoxically [strengthens] the [thing] it appears to [weaken], because it shifts [attention] from [A] to [B]
patterns.explanation: 동명사 주어로 행위를 정의한 뒤, 역설(paradoxically)을 핵심으로 놓고, because절에서 "from A to B" 구조로 메커니즘을 설명하는 패턴. 글의 중심 논지를 담은 문장이다.
examples: Showing vulnerability is a form of courage that paradoxically earns the respect it seems to risk, because it shifts perception from seeing weakness to recognizing authenticity.
Asking questions is a form of intelligence that paradoxically demonstrates the knowledge it appears to lack, because it shifts the conversation from assumption to inquiry.

drillQuestions.question: Naming limits is a form of intellectual _____.
This honesty paradoxically _____ the argument.
It shifts attention from looking for hidden _____ to engaging with substance.
"Paradoxically" means the result is the _____ of what you expect.

sentences.notes: 글의 핵심 역설을 가장 직접적으로 진술하는 문장. "paradoxically strengthens the argument it appears to weaken"이 글 전체의 테제를 압축한다. "from looking for hidden flaws to engaging with the substance"의 전환이 메커니즘을 설명한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Naming limits is a form of intellectual honesty
koreanAlignment.ko: 한계를 명명하는 것은 지적 정직의 한 형태인데

koreanAlignment.en: that paradoxically strengthens the argument
it appears to weaken
because it shifts the audience's attention
from looking for hidden flaws to engaging with the substance of the claim
koreanAlignment.ko: 숨겨진 결함을 찾는 것에서 주장의 본질에 참여하는 것으로

pronunciationNotes: paradoxically: /ˌpærəˈdɑːksɪkli/ — 세 번째 음절에 강세. "패러닥시클리"에 가깝다.
intellectual: /ˌɪntəˈlektʃuəl/ — 세 번째 음절에 강세. "인텔렉추얼"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: When a writer says "I am not arguing that X applies in all cases — only that it applies in the specific context I have described," the reader no longer needs to generate counterexamples, because the writer has already done that work.
sentences.translation: 글쓴이가 "X가 모든 경우에 적용된다고 주장하는 것이 아니라 — 내가 기술한 특정 맥락에서만 적용된다"라고 말하면, 독자는 더 이상 반례를 생성할 필요가 없는데, 글쓴이가 이미 그 작업을 했기 때문이다.

grammarElements.text: When a writer says "I am not arguing that X applies in all cases — only that it applies in the specific context I have described,"
grammarElements.label: 시간 부사절 (직접 인용 포함)

grammarElements.text: because the writer has already done that work
grammarElements.label: because 이유절 (현재완료)

vocabulary.example: A good argument anticipates counterexamples.
The argument applies only in a specific context.
Readers naturally generate objections as they read.

expressions.usage: 이전에 필요했던 행위가 불필요해졌음을 나타내는 표현
현재완료로 과거에 완료된 행위의 현재적 결과를 강조하는 표현

patterns.pattern: When [A] says "[qualified claim]," [B] no longer needs to [counter-action], because [A] has already done that work
patterns.explanation: 직접 인용으로 한정된 주장의 예시를 보여준 뒤, 그 결과로 상대방의 반론 부담이 사라진다는 실용적 효과를 "no longer needs to"와 "already done that work"으로 설명하는 패턴.
examples: When a doctor says "this treatment works well for mild cases but has not been tested for severe ones," the patient no longer needs to worry about overpromising, because the doctor has already done that work.
When a coach says "this strategy is effective against slow defenses but risky against fast ones," the player no longer needs to discover the limit, because the coach has already done that work.

drillQuestions.question: The reader no longer needs to generate _____.
"No longer needs to" means the action is now _____.
The writer "has already done that work" means the writer _____ the limits first.
The quoted statement uses "not ... all cases — only" to _____ the claim.

sentences.notes: 직접 인용이 한정된 주장의 모범적 표현을 보여주고, "no longer needs to generate counterexamples"가 그 실용적 효과를 명시한다. "the writer has already done that work"이 한계 인정이 선제적 방어임을 보여주는 간결한 결론이다.

koreanAlignment.en: When a writer says
"I am not arguing that X applies in all cases — only that it applies in the specific context I have described,"
koreanAlignment.ko: "X가 모든 경우에 적용된다고 주장하는 것이 아니라 — 내가 기술한 특정 맥락에서만 적용된다"

koreanAlignment.en: the reader no longer needs to generate counterexamples
koreanAlignment.ko: 독자는 더 이상 반례를 생성할 필요가 없는데

koreanAlignment.en: because the writer has already done that work
koreanAlignment.ko: 글쓴이가 이미 그 작업을 했기 때문이다

pronunciationNotes: counterexamples: /ˌkaʊntərɪɡˈzæmpəlz/ — 네 번째 음절에 강세. "카운터리그잼플즈"에 가깝다. counter-와 examples가 합쳐진 복합어.
generate: /ˈdʒenəreɪt/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "제너레이트"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: This move also creates space for productive disagreement: instead of debating whether the argument is universally true, both sides can focus on where exactly the boundaries lie and why they matter.
sentences.translation: 이 행동은 또한 생산적 불일치를 위한 공간을 만든다: 주장이 보편적으로 참인지를 논쟁하는 대신, 양측은 경계가 정확히 어디에 있고 왜 중요한지에 집중할 수 있다.

grammarElements.text: instead of debating whether the argument is universally true
grammarElements.label: instead of + 동명사구

grammarElements.text: both sides can focus on where exactly the boundaries lie and why they matter
grammarElements.label: 콜론 뒤 설명절 (간접의문문 2개 병렬)

vocabulary.example: Productive disagreement leads to better ideas.
No argument is universally true.
Both sides debated where the boundaries lie.

expressions.usage: 한계 인정이 더 나은 토론을 가능하게 한다는 뜻의 학술적 표현
"instead of + 동명사" 구조로 이전의 비생산적 방식과 대비하는 표현

patterns.pattern: This move also creates space for [positive outcome]: instead of [unproductive action], both sides can focus on [productive questions]
patterns.explanation: 한계 인정이라는 "move"의 추가적 이점을 제시하고, 콜론 뒤에 "instead of A, B" 구조로 비생산적 논쟁과 생산적 토론을 대비하는 패턴.
examples: This approach also creates space for honest feedback: instead of arguing about who is right, both sides can focus on what specifically needs to change.
This admission also creates space for collaboration: instead of competing over credit, both teams can focus on where their strengths overlap.

drillQuestions.question: This move creates space for productive _____.
"Instead of debating whether" introduces a _____ alternative.
Both sides can focus on where the _____ lie.
"Universally true" means true in _____ cases.

sentences.notes: "This move"가 한계 인정을 전략적 행위로 재정의한다. "productive disagreement"이라는 개념이 불일치를 긍정적으로 재구성하며, "instead of A, B can" 구조가 비생산적 논쟁에서 생산적 토론으로의 전환을 보여준다.

koreanAlignment.en: This move also creates space
for productive disagreement
instead of debating whether the argument is universally true
both sides can focus on where exactly the boundaries lie
koreanAlignment.ko: 양측은 경계가 정확히 어디에 있는지에 집중할 수 있다

koreanAlignment.en: and why they matter

pronunciationNotes: productive: /prəˈdʌktɪv/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "프러덕티브"에 가깝다.
universally: /ˌjuːnɪˈvɜːrsəli/ — 세 번째 음절에 강세. "유니버설리"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: In academic writing, this practice is formalized through "limitations" sections, but the underlying skill transfers to everyday reasoning as well.
sentences.translation: 학술적 글쓰기에서 이 관행은 "한계점" 섹션을 통해 공식화되지만, 기저에 있는 기술은 일상적 추론에도 마찬가지로 전이된다.

grammarElements.text: but the underlying skill transfers to everyday reasoning as well
grammarElements.label: but 대조절

vocabulary.example: The practice has been formalized into official guidelines.
The underlying skill is more important than the format.
Skills learned in one context transfer to others.

expressions.usage: 비공식적 관행이 체계적으로 제도화된 것을 설명하는 학술적 표현

expressions.phrase: transfers to everyday reasoning as well
expressions.usage: 학술적 기술의 일상적 적용 가능성을 강조하는 표현

patterns.pattern: In [formal context], this practice is formalized through [mechanism], but the underlying skill transfers to [informal context] as well
patterns.explanation: 공식적 맥락에서의 관행을 먼저 인정한 뒤, but으로 전환하여 같은 기술이 일상에도 적용된다는 더 넓은 주장으로 확장하는 패턴.
examples: In medicine, this practice is formalized through informed consent procedures, but the underlying skill of transparent communication transfers to personal relationships as well.
In law, this practice is formalized through disclosure requirements, but the underlying principle of openness transfers to business negotiations as well.

drillQuestions.question: In academic writing, naming limits is formalized through "_____ " sections.
"The underlying skill" means the _____ ability beneath the practice.
"Transfers to everyday reasoning" means the skill also works in _____ life.
"As well" at the end means _____.

sentences.notes: 학술에서 일상으로의 전환을 알리는 교량 역할의 문장. "formalized"와 "everyday reasoning"의 대비가 글의 범위를 확장한다. 비교적 짧고 간결한 구조로 논의의 전환점을 명확히 표시한다.

koreanAlignment.en: In academic writing
this practice is formalized through "limitations" sections
but the underlying skill transfers
to everyday reasoning as well

pronunciationNotes: formalized: /ˈfɔːrməlaɪzd/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "포멀라이즈드"에 가깝다.
underlying: /ˌʌndərˈlaɪɪŋ/ — 세 번째 음절에 강세. "언더라이잉"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: A manager who proposes a new workflow and adds "this approach assumes stable demand; if demand spikes, we would need to revisit" demonstrates foresight that colleagues are likely to reward with cooperation rather than resistance.
sentences.translation: 새로운 업무 흐름을 제안하면서 "이 접근법은 안정적 수요를 전제로 합니다; 수요가 급증하면 다시 검토해야 합니다"라고 덧붙이는 관리자는, 동료들이 저항보다는 협력으로 보답할 가능성이 높은 선견지명을 보여준다.

grammarElements.text: A manager who proposes a new workflow and adds "this approach assumes stable demand; if demand spikes, we would need to revisit"
grammarElements.label: 주어 (관계절 + 직접 인용 포함 긴 명사구)

grammarElements.text: that colleagues are likely to reward with cooperation rather than resistance
grammarElements.label: 관계절 (rather than 대조)

vocabulary.example: Naming assumptions in advance shows foresight.
Honest proposals are more likely to earn cooperation.
Overselling often meets resistance from colleagues.
We would need to revisit this plan if conditions change.

expressions.usage: 미래의 문제를 미리 예측하는 능력을 칭찬하는 격식적 표현

expressions.phrase: reward with cooperation rather than resistance
expressions.usage: "rather than"으로 예상되는 부정적 반응 대신 긍정적 반응이 온다는 것을 대비하는 표현

patterns.pattern: [Person] who [proposes X] and adds "[assumption + conditional]" demonstrates [positive quality] that [audience] is likely to reward with [positive response] rather than [negative response]
patterns.explanation: 직접 인용으로 가정과 조건을 명시하는 모범적 행동을 보여주고, 그 결과로 긍정적 반응을 얻는다는 실용적 이점을 "rather than" 대조로 제시하는 패턴.
examples: A consultant who presents a forecast and adds "these numbers assume no regulatory changes; if regulations tighten, margins could shrink" demonstrates humility that clients are likely to reward with trust rather than suspicion.

A team lead who shares a timeline and adds "this schedule assumes full staffing; if someone leaves, we need to adjust" demonstrates realism that stakeholders are likely to reward with support rather than pushback.

drillQuestions.question: The manager demonstrates _____ by naming assumptions.
Colleagues are likely to reward with cooperation rather than _____.
"If demand spikes, we would need to revisit" is a _____ clause.
"This approach assumes stable demand" names an underlying _____.

sentences.notes: 일상적 직장 맥락으로 논의를 확장하는 구체적 사례. 직접 인용이 가정 명시의 모범을 생생히 보여주고, "foresight"와 "cooperation rather than resistance"가 실용적 보상을 강조한다. 앞의 학술적 사례와 병렬을 이룬다.

koreanAlignment.en: A manager who proposes a new workflow
and adds "this approach assumes stable demand; if demand spikes, we would need to revisit"
koreanAlignment.ko: "이 접근법은 안정적 수요를 전제로 합니다; 수요가 급증하면 다시 검토해야 합니다"라고 덧붙이는 관리자는

koreanAlignment.en: demonstrates foresight
that colleagues are likely to reward with cooperation
rather than resistance

pronunciationNotes: foresight: /ˈfɔːrsaɪt/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "포사이트"에 가깝다. fore-는 "미리"를 뜻하는 접두사.
resistance: /rɪˈzɪstəns/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "리지스턴스"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: The reluctance to name limits often stems from a fear that doing so will make the argument appear weak, but the evidence suggests the opposite.
sentences.translation: 한계를 명명하기를 꺼리는 것은 그렇게 하면 주장이 약해 보일 것이라는 두려움에서 비롯되는 경우가 많지만, 증거는 그 반대를 시사한다.

grammarElements.text: a fear that doing so will make the argument appear weak
grammarElements.label: 목적어 (that 동격절 포함)

grammarElements.text: but the evidence suggests the opposite
grammarElements.label: but 대조절

vocabulary.example: His reluctance to apologize made things worse.
The problem stems from a lack of communication.
The evidence suggests a different conclusion.

expressions.usage: 행동의 심리적 원인을 분석할 때 쓰는 격식적 표현
통념을 반박할 때 쓰는 간결하고 강력한 학술적 표현

patterns.pattern: The reluctance to [action] often stems from a fear that [doing so] will [negative consequence], but the evidence suggests the opposite
patterns.explanation: 심리적 장벽(두려움)을 먼저 인정한 뒤, "but the evidence suggests the opposite"로 간결하게 뒤집는 패턴. 통념과 증거의 대비가 핵심.
examples: The reluctance to show emotion often stems from a fear that doing so will make a person appear unprofessional, but the evidence suggests the opposite.
The reluctance to delegate often stems from a fear that doing so will reduce quality, but the evidence suggests the opposite.

drillQuestions.question: The reluctance to name limits stems from a _____.
"Stems from" means _____ from.
The evidence suggests the _____ of the fear.
"Doing so" refers to naming _____.

sentences.notes: 두려움의 심리적 원인을 분석하고 즉시 반박하는 전환 문장. "The reluctance to name limits"가 핵심 장벽을 포착하고, "but the evidence suggests the opposite"가 간결하면서도 결정적인 반론을 제시한다.

koreanAlignment.en: The reluctance to name limits
often stems from a fear
that doing so will make the argument appear weak
koreanAlignment.ko: 그렇게 하면 주장이 약해 보일 것이라는

koreanAlignment.en: but the evidence suggests the opposite

pronunciationNotes: reluctance: /rɪˈlʌktəns/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "릴럭턴스"에 가깝다.
evidence: /ˈevɪdəns/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "에비던스"에 가깝다. 세 음절로 발음.

sentences.original: Studies on persuasion consistently find that two-sided arguments — those that acknowledge counterpoints before addressing them — are more convincing than one-sided arguments, particularly among educated audiences who are already aware of the complexity.
sentences.translation: 설득에 관한 연구들은 양면적 주장 — 반론을 다루기 전에 인정하는 주장 — 이 일면적 주장보다 더 설득력이 있다는 것을 일관되게 발견하는데, 특히 이미 복잡성을 인식하고 있는 교육받은 청중 사이에서 그러하다.

grammarElements.text: that two-sided arguments are more convincing than one-sided arguments
grammarElements.label: that 명사절 (비교급)

grammarElements.text: those that acknowledge counterpoints before addressing them
grammarElements.label: 대시 삽입구 (동격 설명)

grammarElements.text: particularly among educated audiences who are already aware of the complexity
grammarElements.label: 부사구 + 관계절

vocabulary.example: Two-sided arguments acknowledge opposing views.
Acknowledging counterpoints strengthens your case.
Balanced arguments are more convincing.
Educated audiences are aware of the complexity.

expressions.usage: 여러 연구의 반복적 결과를 요약할 때 쓰는 학술적 표현
효과가 특히 강한 대상을 한정하는 학술적 표현

patterns.pattern: Studies on [topic] consistently find that [two-sided approach] — [definition] — is more [positive quality] than [one-sided approach], particularly among [audience type]
patterns.explanation: 경험적 연구 결과를 인용하여 양면적 접근의 우월성을 주장하고, 대시 삽입구로 용어를 정의하며, "particularly among"으로 효과가 가장 큰 대상을 명시하는 패턴.
examples: Studies on negotiation consistently find that proposals that include trade-offs — those that offer concessions before demanding them — are more successful than take-it-or-leave-it offers, particularly among experienced negotiators who expect reciprocity.

Studies on teaching consistently find that interactive methods — those that invite student participation before delivering content — are more effective than lecture-only approaches, particularly among advanced learners who already possess foundational knowledge.

drillQuestions.question: Two-sided arguments acknowledge _____ before addressing them.
Two-sided arguments are more _____ than one-sided ones.
This effect is particularly strong among _____ audiences.
"Consistently find" means the result appears _____ across studies.

sentences.notes: 경험적 증거로 핵심 주장을 뒷받침하는 문장. 대시 삽입구가 "two-sided arguments"를 정의하고, "particularly among educated audiences"가 효과의 범위를 한정한다. "one-sided"와 "two-sided"의 대비가 논지를 명확히 한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Studies on persuasion consistently find that
two-sided arguments
those that acknowledge counterpoints before addressing them
are more convincing than one-sided arguments
koreanAlignment.ko: 일면적 주장보다 더 설득력이 있다는 것을

koreanAlignment.en: particularly among educated audiences who are already aware of the complexity
koreanAlignment.ko: 특히 이미 복잡성을 인식하고 있는 교육받은 청중 사이에서

pronunciationNotes: persuasion: /pərˈsweɪʒən/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "퍼스웨이전"에 가깝다. sion이 /ʒən/으로 발음.
counterpoints: /ˈkaʊntərpɔɪnts/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "카운터포인츠"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: The skill, then, is not merely rhetorical; it reflects a genuine understanding that knowledge is bounded and that the most dangerous conclusions are the ones that pretend otherwise.
sentences.translation: 따라서 이 기술은 단지 수사적인 것이 아니다; 그것은 지식이 한정되어 있으며 가장 위험한 결론은 그렇지 않은 척하는 결론이라는 진정한 이해를 반영한다.

grammarElements.text: it reflects a genuine understanding that knowledge is bounded
grammarElements.label: 세미콜론 뒤 절 (that 동격절)

grammarElements.text: and that the most dangerous conclusions are the ones that pretend otherwise
grammarElements.label: 병렬 that 절 (관계절 포함)

vocabulary.example: The skill goes beyond rhetorical technique.
All human knowledge is bounded by experience.
The most dangerous claims pretend to explain everything.

expressions.usage: "merely"로 가벼운 해석을 부정하고, 더 깊은 의미가 있음을 암시하는 표현
"otherwise"가 앞의 주장(knowledge is bounded)과 반대되는 태도를 가리키는 표현

patterns.pattern: The skill, then, is not merely [surface quality]; it reflects a genuine understanding that [deep truth A] and that [deep truth B]
patterns.explanation: 삽입 부사 "then"으로 결론적 전환을 표시하고, 세미콜론 뒤에 기술의 깊은 의미를 두 개의 병렬 that절로 풀어내는 패턴. 표면과 깊이의 대비가 핵심.
examples: The habit, then, is not merely disciplinary; it reflects a genuine understanding that attention is finite and that the most productive hours are the ones spent deliberately.
The practice, then, is not merely polite; it reflects a genuine understanding that perspectives differ and that the most inclusive decisions are the ones that incorporate dissent.

drillQuestions.question: The skill is not merely _____ — it goes deeper.
It reflects a genuine understanding that knowledge is _____.
The most dangerous conclusions are the ones that _____ otherwise.
"Then" in this sentence signals a _____.

sentences.notes: 핵심 주장을 인식론적 차원으로 격상시키는 문장. "not merely rhetorical"이 표면적 해석을 거부하고, "knowledge is bounded"와 "pretend otherwise"의 대비가 지적 정직의 철학적 근거를 제시한다. 삽입 부사 "then"이 결론으로의 전환을 부드럽게 표시한다.

koreanAlignment.en: The skill, then, is not merely rhetorical
koreanAlignment.ko: 따라서 이 기술은 단지 수사적인 것이 아니다

koreanAlignment.en: it reflects a genuine understanding
that knowledge is bounded
and that the most dangerous conclusions
are the ones that pretend otherwise

pronunciationNotes: rhetorical: /rɪˈtɔːrɪkəl/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "리토리컬"에 가깝다. rh-는 /r/로 발음.
bounded: /ˈbaʊndɪd/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "바운디드"에 가깝다. bound + -ed.

sentences.original: Good arguments do not claim to be complete — they claim to be honest, and they trust the audience to recognize the difference.
sentences.translation: 좋은 주장은 완전하다고 주장하지 않는다 — 정직하다고 주장하며, 청중이 그 차이를 인식할 것을 신뢰한다.

grammarElements.text: and they trust the audience to recognize the difference
grammarElements.label: 병렬절 (trust + 목적격 보어)

vocabulary.example: No argument can claim to be complete.
Honest arguments name their own limits.
Audiences can recognize the difference between honesty and arrogance.

expressions.phrase: do not claim to be complete
expressions.usage: 겸양과 지적 정직의 태도를 나타내는 표현

expressions.phrase: trust the audience to recognize the difference
expressions.usage: "trust + 목적어 + to 부정사" 구조로 청중에 대한 존중을 표현

patterns.pattern: Good [nouns] do not claim to be [A] — they claim to be [B], and they trust [audience] to recognize the difference
patterns.explanation: 대시로 구분된 "not A — but B" 구조에 "trust the audience"를 추가하여, 정직한 주장이 청중의 판단력을 존중한다는 결론을 제시하는 패턴. 글 전체의 핵심을 한 문장으로 압축한다.
examples: Good leaders do not claim to be infallible — they claim to be accountable, and they trust their teams to recognize the difference.
Good teachers do not claim to know everything — they claim to be curious, and they trust their students to recognize the difference.

drillQuestions.question: Good arguments do not claim to be _____.
Instead, they claim to be _____.
They trust the audience to _____ the difference.
The contrast is between completeness and _____.

sentences.notes: 글의 마지막 문장으로, 전체 논지를 "complete"와 "honest"의 대비로 압축한다. "trust the audience to recognize the difference"가 청중에 대한 존중을 표현하며, 글쓴이의 논증 자체가 자기 주장을 실천하는 메타적 구조를 완성한다. 간결하면서도 울림 있는 결론이다.

koreanAlignment.en: Good arguments do not claim to be complete
they claim to be honest
and they trust the audience
to recognize the difference

pronunciationNotes: recognize: /ˈrekəɡnaɪz/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "레커그나이즈"에 가깝다. g는 묵음이 아님에 주의.
audience: /ˈɔːdiəns/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "오디언스"에 가깝다. au가 /ɔː/로 발음.
